exceptions.reason: reportable execution TXN-3 has no matching submitted report
submitted report R-6 for TXN-6 has no reportable source (no source execution for this transaction_ref)
report R-2 submitted 48.5h after execution, exceeds 24h deadline
report R-4 price=55.75 differs from source price=55.0
report R-7 is missing mandatory field(s): buyer_id
report R-7 field reporting_firm_lei value does not match required LEI format
report R-8 status is 'rejected' and is not yet resolved

false_positive_checks: confirm no reporting exemption, waiver, or deferral applies to the flagged transaction
normalize timezones and cut-off calendars before treating a report as late
confirm a correction is not already in flight before treating a reject as unresolved
confirm the source-of-record field is itself correct before treating a mismatch as a report defect
confirm the reference-data (LEI/ISIN/MIC) snapshot used is the effective one
disclaimer: Quality-control findings only; not a compliance determination. No regulatory report has been submitted, amended, cancelled, or suppressed.
narrative: Quality-control review of the MiFIR-RTS22 batch as of 2026-07-17 examined 6 reportable executions against 6 submitted reports (5 matched). It surfaced 7 exceptions: a reportable execution with no report (TXN-3), a report with no reportable source (TXN-6), a reported price that differs from the source of record (TXN-4), a report submitted after the timeliness deadline (TXN-2), a mandatory field left empty and an identifier that does not match the required LEI format (TXN-7), and an unresolved reject (TXN-8). Suggested remediation priority: Blocking. Each exception cites its source and reported evidence. These are quality-control findings for human remediation review; a reporting-operations analyst decides disposition and any downstream routing. Run the false-positive checks before escalating.
